author: dsanders11
comment_body: I definitely see the benefits of the trailing commas, but I'll make two arguments against. Wall of text to follow, but I don't have a strong opinion either way.  First, most of the code does not have a trailing comma, so it is inconsistent in the codebase. There's only a few in this commit that I found, which shows that the majority of them don't have a trailing comma. Regardless of preferred style it would be nice if it was consistent.  Second, other languages often don't allow trailing commas. This may seem trivial, but I'm currently porting as much of ZXing as I can to JavaScript, and the two languages are close enough that sometimes it's just a matter of tweaking some syntax and keywords. ~Trailing commas in arrays aren't allowed in JavaScript, though, so I hit that a few times because it's easy to not see visually when porting hundreds of lines~. So I'd make the argument that it makes the code ever so slightly more accessible for porting to other languages without the trailing commas. Obviously language-specific features shouldn't be avoided for this reason, but in this case its something trivial.  Anyway, not a strong opinion, but there's my rationale. I can remove the comma relate edits if you'd still like to keep the trailing commas.
